extra.message: User input from form.registration_code.data is passed to validate_token() and interpolated via f-string into a raw SQL query: text(f"SELECT id, code FROM {RegistrationCode.__tablename__} WHERE code = '{code}'"). This allows SQL injection through the registration_code field.

User input from request.args.get('search') is interpolated via f-string into a raw SQL LIKE clause: text(f"text like '%{search_param}%'"), which is passed to SQLAlchemy filter(). This allows SQL injection through the search query parameter.

request.path is user-controlled and included in an f-string passed to render_template_string(). An attacker can request a URL like /{{config}} to execute Jinja2 template code, leading to Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) and potential remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: pickle.loads() is called on data decoded from the user-controlled 'preferences' cookie (loads(b64decode(preferences))). An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload in the cookie to achieve arbitrary code execution on the server.

urlopen(url) is called with a user-supplied URL from the image upload form without any validation or restriction. An attacker can supply internal network URLs (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/), file:// URLs, or other internal resources, enabling Server-Side Request Forgery.

Note content is rendered with the | safe filter: {{ note.text | safe }}. Since users can create notes with arbitrary HTML via the CKEditor (which allows rich text/HTML input), this enables stored Cross-Site Scripting. Any user viewing the home page will execute the injected script.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The render_table macro is called with safe_columns=['text'], which disables HTML escaping for the 'text' column. Since note text contains user-created HTML from CKEditor, this enables stored XSS in the search results table.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The render_table macro is called with safe_columns=['text'], which disables HTML escaping for the 'text' column. Since note text contains user-created HTML from CKEditor, this enables stored XSS in the personal notes table.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The get_personal_notes endpoint at /accounts/<int:user_id>/notes queries notes by the user_id URL parameter without verifying that user_id matches current_user.id. Any authenticated user can view any other user's private notes by changing the user_id in the URL.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The update_account route at POST /account lacks the @login_required decorator. Any unauthenticated user can submit a POST request to modify account information, including email, password, and is_admin fields.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The toggle_darkmode route at POST /darkmode lacks the @login_required decorator. Any unauthenticated user can toggle the dark mode preference, and since this handler deserializes cookies via pickle.loads(), it increases the attack surface for the deserialization vulnerability.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The delete_note endpoint deletes a note by note_id without checking that note.user_id matches current_user.id. Any authenticated user can delete any note belonging to any other user by guessing or enumerating note IDs.

Flask secret key is hardcoded as 'super secret key'. This key is used for signing session cookies. An attacker who knows this key can forge session cookies, potentially gaining unauthorized access to any account including admin.

The update_account function updates current_user.__dict__ with all form fields except password (filtered_values = {k:v for k,v in form.data.items() if v is not None and k != 'password'}). The AccountForm includes an is_admin BooleanField, so any user can submit is_admin=True in the POST request to escalate their privileges to admin. There is no server-side authorization check on this field.

AccountForm inherits from wtforms.Form instead of FlaskForm from flask_wtf. This means no CSRF token is generated or validated. An attacker can craft a cross-origin POST request to /account to modify a victim's email, password, or is_admin status without their consent.

NoteForm inherits from wtforms.Form instead of FlaskForm from flask_wtf. This means no CSRF token is generated or validated. An attacker can craft a cross-origin POST request to /notes to create notes on behalf of a victim user.

LoginForm inherits from wtforms.Form instead of FlaskForm from flask_wtf. This means no CSRF token is generated or validated, allowing login CSRF attacks where an attacker can log a victim into an attacker-controlled account.
RegistrationForm inherits from wtforms.Form instead of FlaskForm from flask_wtf. This means no CSRF token is generated or validated, allowing cross-origin account creation.

ImageForm inherits from wtforms.Form instead of FlaskForm from flask_wtf. This means no CSRF token is generated or validated. An attacker can craft a cross-origin POST request to /account/image to change a victim's profile image to an attacker-controlled URL.

The SQLAlchemy engine is created with echo=True, which logs all SQL statements and their parameters to stdout. This includes sensitive data such as user passwords (during hash comparisons), registration codes, and other query parameters, leading to information disclosure in logs.

cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
